title: How Browser Image Compression Works
excerpt: Learn how quality, dimensions, and browser encoders affect image file size.
body: Browser image compression uses built-in encoders to redraw pixels and export a smaller file.
The best results usually come from resizing oversized images before lowering quality.
For web publishing, WebP often gives an excellent balance of detail and size.

title: Image SEO Checklist for Faster Pages
excerpt: A practical checklist for naming, sizing, compressing, and publishing images.
body: Use descriptive filenames and alt text that match the image context.
Serve images at the dimensions your layout actually needs.
Compress hero images carefully because they often affect LCP.

title: WebP vs JPG vs PNG: Which Format Should You Use?
excerpt: Choose the right format for photos, graphics, transparency, and compatibility.
body: JPG is still useful for broad compatibility and photographic images.
PNG is best for lossless graphics and transparency.
WebP is a strong default for modern websites when browser support fits your audience.
